svg.title: Hadamard States by Pseudo-Differential Calculus (Gérard-Wrochna)
svg.desc: A schematic placeholder diagram for the Gérard-Wrochna 2014 direct pseudo-differential construction of Hadamard states. A single globally hyperbolic spacetime (M, g) is drawn as a curved cylinder with a horizontal Cauchy slice Sigma marked across the middle. On Sigma the operator epsilon, a pseudo-differential parametrix of the square root of the spatial operator D_h = minus Laplacian plus mass squared plus potential, is constructed via Seeley calculus. The two-point function omega_2 is built from the pair (epsilon, sigma) of Cauchy-data covariance and symplectic form; its wave-front set is constrained on T*M minus zero to the future-pointing half of the bicharacteristic relation, the Radzikowski criterion. No deformation argument is used: the construction is intrinsic to (M, g).
svg.text: Gérard-Wrochna 2014: Hadamard state from a Ψ¹-pseudo-differential parametrix ε of √(D_h) on Σ.
Direct construction on a single (M, g); no deformation, no auxiliary ultrastatic reference.
η = ½ ∫_Σ (u₀ ε u₀ + u₁ ε⁻¹ u₁) dvol_h ⇒ WF(ω₂) = future-pointing half of bichar. (Radzikowski)
Gérard-Wrochna 2014 Comm. Math. Phys. 325 713; Gérard-Wrochna 2017 Ann. Henri Poincaré 18 2715; Seeley 1967.
